meta.title: Grammar System — Architecture, Compounds, Roadmap, Status
meta.description: How the hand-rolled Earley grammar fits together, how compounds are handled, where we are, and the recovered open questions.

props.title: How it all fits together
props.subtitle: Our own Earley engine (~1329 LOC, zero parser deps). Reverse-Earley repair is intrinsic, not a mode. Compounds = chart islands + weighted-interval tiling.
props.meta: S0–S5 complete · W1 landed · W2 running · 4 of ~38 families

props.content: You didn't roll a regex sliding window — the Earley chart already IS the windowed span search (a well-formed substring table built by dynamic programming). This page shows the architecture, how compounds work on that chart, the live roadmap, and the questions that were getting lost.

props.label: Architecture
props.title: Did we roll our own? Yes — and it's layered, not spaghetti

props.content:
`modules/intent/grammar/` is a hand-built Earley engine — **no lark, no nltk, no spacy**. Deliberate: the no-new-deps rule plus full control over the priced repair model that off-the-shelf parsers don't give you. It's already split by responsibility:

- **grammar_ir** — immutable domain types (Production, Symbol), pure, no IO
- **scanner** — tokenize + zero-cost normalization + match costs
- **lexemes** — `LexemeResolver` Protocol + registry (strategy pattern: one resolver per slot family)
- **template_compiler** — authority rows → productions (fail-closed)
- **earley** — the chart parser (predict/scan/complete) + priced repair + partials
- **engine** — `GrammarEngine` factory (fail-closed) + `candidates_for`
- **semantics** — ParseResult → typed Candidate projection
- **provider** — `GrammarCandidateProvider`, the pipeline adapter

**Reverse-Earley is intrinsic, not a setting.** Every parse already emits a repair ledger — that ledger *is* the corrected-transcript diff. Affix pricing (W2) and governed pricing (W3) extend the cost model; they are not separate modes.

props.label: Layers
props.title: Ports, adapters, and where recognizers plug in

props.caption: Domain is pure; the provider is the only thing that crosses into the pipeline. Config is a value object, so strict + loose profiles are just instances.
props.content:
flowchart TD
  TPL["authority templates"] --> COMP["template_compiler
(fail-closed)"]
  COMP --> IR["grammar_ir
Production/Symbol"]
  IR --> CHART["earley chart
predict/scan/complete
+ priced repair"]
  SCAN["scanner
normalize + costs"] --> CHART
  LEX["lexemes
resolver registry
(strategy per slot)"] --> CHART
  CFG["ParserConfig
(strict | loose split-detection)"] --> CHART
  CHART --> SEM["semantics
ParseResult to Candidate"]
  SEM --> ENG["GrammarEngine
factory"]
  ENG --> PROV["GrammarCandidateProvider
(adapter)"]
  PROV --> PIPE["PolicyChain pipeline
(only selector)"]

props.label: Build for multiple
props.title: The one abstraction to add (and the one to skip)

props.content:
**Multiple configs already work.** `ParserConfig` is a value object, so the strict candidate parser and the loose split-detection parser (your compound idea) are just two instances — no new abstraction.

**Multiple recognizer *types* need one thin port.** Today `GrammarEngine` is concrete. To hold grammar *and* a future regex spotter without spaghetti, define a small `Recognizer` Protocol — `(text, config) → span hypotheses` — and make Earley the first implementer. Decision: **add the port when W6 lands** (the splitter is the first place that holds more than one recognizer); build only the port + Earley adapter, **not** the regex implementer (deferred). The seam exists, clean, and the regex spotter drops in later with zero refactor.

props.label: Compounds
props.title: How compounds are handled — no 'first n', no separate WFST

props.content:
Two steps, both on the same chart:

1. **W2 island parsing** lets the chart accept a **sub-span** — a command embedded in verbiage — each with intent + slots. The Earley chart already enumerates every `(start, end)` span as dynamic programming, so **the chart IS the well-formed substring table**. There's no separate WFST and no manual 'take the first n words' loop — that sliding window is the brute-force form of what the chart computes in one pass.
2. **W6 tiling** selects the best **non-overlapping** set of islands via **weighted interval scheduling** (max-weight independent set on an interval graph), pricing the connector tokens between islands. The tiling runs under a **looser split-detection config** that is structurally unable to emit a selectable candidate — it only proposes boundaries, in shadow.

So it's **Earley (fills the chart) + weighted-interval tiling on top**, not 'WFST + Earley' as two components.

props.caption: The chart finds every command island; weighted-interval scheduling picks the non-overlapping cover; it runs in shadow next to the keyword splitter.
props.content:
flowchart TD
  U["turn left 270 descend and maintain 3000 contact tower 119.3"]
  U --> CH["Earley chart (the WFST)\nall spans, DP — W2 islands"]
  CH --> I1["island: turn_direction_heading\nspan 1-3"]
  CH --> I2["island: descend_and_maintain\nspan 4-7"]
  CH --> I3["island: contact_facility_frequency\nspan 8-11"]
  I1 --> WIS["weighted interval scheduling\nmax-weight non-overlapping cover\n(W6, loose split profile)"]
  I2 --> WIS
  I3 --> WIS
  WIS --> PROP["segmentation proposal\n+ per-island intents"]
  PROP --> SHADOW["SHADOW: measured vs keyword splitter\nno split decision changed"]

props.label: Recovered
props.title: Your questions that were getting lost — now tracked

props.label: BIGGEST GAP
props.title: Expand beyond 4 families
props.content: ~38 families exist; we cover 4 (~11%). NOT a workstream yet. It's the **next phase after W1–W6** — each new family then inherits a working repair/promotion/measurement stack. Sequence by failure-cluster size. Do not mass-compile before the machinery + guards exist.

props.label: PARTIAL
props.title: Richer candidate evidence
props.content: More than row_id + intent. ParseResult already has slots, score, repair ledger, span; trace now has repair_op_count. Slot-level provenance + bands enrich incrementally inside W3/W4 — not one big rewrite.

props.label: FOLLOW-UP
props.title: Confirmation loop
props.content: Repaired commands escalate to needs-confirmation; parse ATC's reply as the next turn. Separate E2E plan (session FSM + pending-command state). Blocks repaired-band promotion.

props.label: DEFERRED
props.title: Recognizer ensemble
props.content: Grammar + regex spotters. Port defined at W6 (Earley first implementer); regex impl deferred by you. Seam built clean so it drops in later with zero refactor.
